input.system: You are a customer support classifier. Read the customer email and respond ONLY with a JSON object of the form: {"category": one of "billing", "technical", "feature_request", "complaint", "other", "reply": a 2-sentence acknowledgement that confirms receipt and indicates investigation without committing to specific outcomes}. Do not include any other text.
input.user: Hi, I just got my latest invoice from Acme Co for £150 but my plan is supposed to be £99 per month. Can you check what the extra £51 is for? My account is under customer@example.com. Thanks, Customer A

tier_2_judge.criteria: Reply acknowledges the issue and indicates investigation without committing to specific outcomes (e.g. without promising a refund, without asserting the charge is correct or incorrect).

input.system: You are a customer support classifier. Read the customer email and respond ONLY with a JSON object of the form: {"category": one of "billing", "technical", "feature_request", "complaint", "other", "reply": a 2-sentence acknowledgement that confirms receipt and indicates investigation without committing to specific outcomes}. Do not include any other text.
input.user: Hello, I've been trying to integrate the Acme Co webhook API into our internal workflow for the past two days and the test events keep arriving with malformed JSON — specifically the `timestamp` field comes through as a string in some events and a Unix integer in others, which is breaking our parser. We're on the standard plan, account customer@example.com. This is blocking a release we have scheduled for tomorrow morning. Could someone from engineering take a look? — Customer A

tier_2_judge.criteria: Reply acknowledges the issue and indicates investigation without committing to specific outcomes (e.g. without promising a fix timeline, without asserting the bug exists or doesn't).

metadata.notes: Technical bug report with urgency cue; classification should be 'technical' not 'complaint'

input.system: You are a customer support classifier. Read the customer email and respond ONLY with a JSON object of the form: {"category": one of "billing", "technical", "feature_request", "complaint", "other", "reply": a 2-sentence acknowledgement that confirms receipt and indicates investigation without committing to specific outcomes}. Do not include any other text.
input.user: To whom it may concern. I have been a customer of Acme Co for over four years and over the last six months the service has noticeably degraded. The dashboard takes 8-12 seconds to load most mornings, the export-to-CSV button silently fails about one in three attempts, and when I raised a ticket on 12 March the reply came back two weeks later with a templated apology and no concrete action. I am not asking for compensation but I am letting you know that the team here is actively evaluating moving to a competitor. — Customer A, customer@example.com

tier_2_judge.criteria: Reply acknowledges the breadth of issues raised and indicates a serious internal review without committing to specific outcomes (no refund offers, no SLA promises, no claims about retention). Tone should match the escalated nature of the message.

metadata.notes: Escalated multi-issue email. Designed to test disambiguation between 'complaint' and 'technical'/'feature_request'. Expected: 'complaint' because the customer is expressing dissatisfaction holistically rather than requesting a specific fix or feature.
